{"additions":62215,"baseRefName":"main","body":"## Summary\n\n- **Target color feature** — new target color input with multi-format display, format-matched copy feedback, and inline format display ([TASK-0046](board/)).\n- **Component refactor** — flattened `app/src/components/` structure into per-component folders with `index.ts` barrels; extracted `LightnessChart`, `SaturationCurve`, `EffectiveSaturation`, etc.\n- **Design tokens** — collapsed text colors from 6 to 4 (`primary`/`secondary`/`muted`/`faint`); brightened About tokens; added contrast audit script.\n- **Tooling** — added Prettier (`prettier-plugin-tailwindcss`), ESLint (`eslint-plugin-better-tailwindcss`, React, import resolver), Knip (local schema), size/complexity rules; colocated configs under `app/`; renamed `check` → `verify` and added `verify:fix`/`verify:strict`; added dev orchestration scripts with configurable port.\n- **Docs** — added EPIC-0013 (SSG for crawlers) and EPIC-0014 (target color UX refinements) with supporting tasks; relocated `CLAUDE.md` to `.claude/`.\n- **Codacy** — added local Codacy CLI config.\n\n## Test plan\n\n- [ ] `npm run verify` passes (lint, format, knip, typecheck)\n- [ ] `npm run build` produces `app/dist/`\n- [ ] `npm run dev` — target color input works end-to-end (input, format switching, copy feedback)\n- [ ] Visually spot-check About view and palette grid for token regressions\n- [ ] Verify Codacy CLI runs   {
    "from": "feature-reviewer",
    "text": "PR #5 target color feature review complete. Reviewed: `TargetSwatch.tsx` (326 lines, new), `TargetFormats.tsx` (55 lines, new), `lib/colors.ts` (90 lines added), `paletteStore.ts` (+32 lines), `types/palette.ts` (formatting only).\n\n---\n\n## Critical\n\nNone.\n\n---\n\n## High\n\n**`lib/colors.ts:71` — `color.rgb()` returns floats; output is invalid CSS**\n`formatTargetColor` for `\"rgb\"` format does:\n```ts\nconst [r, g, b] = color.rgb();\nreturn `rgb(${r},${g},${b})`;\n```\nchroma.js `.rgb()` returns raw floats. For non-pure-hue colors (e.g. `hsl(30, 50%, 50%)`), this produces `rgb(191.25,143.75000000000003,63.75)` — invalid CSS. Browsers will reject or misinterpret it, and copy/paste to any tool will fail silently.\n**Fix:** `return \\`rgb(${Math.round(r)},${Math.round(g)},${Math.round(b)})\\`;`\nConfidence: 90\n\n---\n\n## Important\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:80–92` — Text input has no accessible label**\nThe `<input>` inside `ColorInput` has no `aria-label` or associated `<label>`. Only a `placeholder` attribute. Placeholder disappears on type and screen readers don't treat it as a label. Keyboard/AT users won't know what the field is for.\n**Fix:** Add `aria-label=\"Enter target color (hex, rgb, hsl, oklch)\"` to the input.\nConfidence: 85\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:286–294` — Clear button invisible to keyboard focus**\nThe clear-target button uses `opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100` with no `focus-visible:opacity-100`. It's in the DOM and focusable, but fully invisible when tabbed to — a WCAG 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance) failure.\n**Fix:** Add `focus-visible:opacity-100` to the button's class list.\nConfidence: 85\n\n**`TargetFormats.tsx:27` — `opacity-55` container opacity violates token guidelines**\nCLAUDE.md: \"Use design tokens instead of hardcoded HSL or opacity tricks.\" The \"drifted\" state is implemented with `opacity-55` on the whole container, which compounds with existing text token opacities and may push `text-text-faint` below WCAG AA contrast. Should use lower-tier tokens on individual elements (e.g. swap `text-text-secondary` → `text-text-faint`) rather than blanket opacity.\nConfidence: 80\n\n**`TargetSwatch.tsx:231–244` — Canvas picker has no touch/pointer support**\nMouse events only (`mousemove`, `mouseup` on document, `onMouseDown` on canvas). Touch users and stylus users cannot drag the color picker. The rest of the UI (sliders via Radix) is touch-accessible, so this is an inconsistency.\n**Fix:** Replace with pointer events (`onPointerDown`, `pointermove`, `pointerup`) which unify mouse + touch + stylus.\nConfidence: 80\n\n---\n\n## Nit (below threshold, flagging anyway)\n\n**`lib/colors.ts:69` — HSL output lacks spaces after commas**\n`hsl(${hue},${Math.round(saturation * 100)}%,50%)` is technically valid CSS but the canonical format uses spaces. More visibly: if a user types `hsl(120, 75%, 50%)` (with spaces), the copy-feedback line shows `hsl(120,75%,50%)` (without), creating a jarring inconsistency.\nConfidence: 70 (not reported as a finding, just calling it out)\n\n---\n\n## State Management\n\nNo issues. `setTarget` correctly updates both live hue/saturation and target snapshot in a single `set()` call — no partial-state race. `clearTarget` is symmetric. No stale closures visible.\n\n## Color Parsing\n\n`parseTargetColor` logic is sound: oklch is pre-parsed (chroma.js can't handle it), format detection is correct for hex/hsl/rgb/named, NaN guards on achromatic colors are handled. The \"named\" format fallback to hex in `formatTargetColor` is intentional and correct.\n\n---\n\nTask #1 marked complete.",
    "summary": "PR #5 target color feature review findings",
